title: Lathyrus formosus
intro: Lathyrus formosus, also known as the Formosa pea or Formosa sweet pea, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lathyrus formosus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lathyrus formosus is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to take advantage of its health benefits, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Lathyrus formosus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Lathyrus, which comprises several species of pea vines. The plant is native to the mountainous regions of Taiwan and is known for its showy, fragrant flowers. The classification of Lathyrus formosus is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, and the order Fabales. Its specific classification is Lathyrus formosus (Miq.) Bunge, indicating the plant's botanical name and classification hierarchy.
uses: Lathyrus formosus has been traditionally used in various forms of medicine. The plant is known to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can be beneficial in treating various health conditions. Its extracts have been used to treat gastrointestinal issues, reduce fever, and alleviate pain. Furthermore, Lathyrus formosus has been used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. However, its medicinal uses may vary depending on the region and cultural practices.

side_effects: Lathyrus formosus is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. However, there is limited information available on the side effects of consuming Lathyrus formosus, and more research is needed to fully understand its safety profile. Some studies suggest that consuming the plant's seeds in large quantities may cause neurolathyrism, a condition characterized by muscle weakness and paralysis. The exact mechanisms and potential side effects of Lathyrus formosus are not yet fully understood and require further investigation.
